name: createContextHook
typeDeclarations:
import type { ReactNode } from 'react';

type Value = ReturnType<Hook>;
description: Hook that runs inside a Provider; Provider accepts params (the hook's arguments). Use when you need a scoped instance per subtree.
examples: const { Provider, use } = createContextHook(useMediaQuery); <Provider params={["(max-width: 768px)"]}> <Component /> </Provider> const matches = use();
warning: For complex interfaces, we strongly recommend using state management solutions outside of React like createStore, reatom, effector, or zustand instead of context

parameters.name: useHook
parameters.description: - The hook to run in the Provider (e.g. useMediaQuery)

source.source: * @param useHook - The hook to run in the Provider (e.g. useMediaQuery)

tokens.name: useHook
tokens.description: - The hook to run in the Provider (e.g. useMediaQuery)

returns.description: Provider takes params; use() returns the value and must be used inside Provider

source.source: * @returns { Provider, use } — Provider takes params; use() returns the value and must be used inside Provider

tokens.description: Provider takes params; use() returns the value and must be used inside Provider
